vulnerabilities.summary: Sensitive debug log file with no access controls - exposes session IDs, cookies, OAuth codes, and request data

what_is_logged: All GET parameters including OAuth authorization codes ($_GET array)
Additional debug messages via logd() function
guards_checked: web.config (IIS): EXISTS but contains NO rules blocking .log files
Apache/Nginx config files: NOT FOUND in repository
PHP authorization checks: NONE - file is written before any auth
robots.txt: Does not protect files from HTTP access
details.accessibility: WEB-ACCESSIBLE - No server configuration blocks .log file access
details.file_current_state: Does not exist yet - will be created on first request to /submissions.php
details.gitignore_status: Listed in .gitignore at line 9, so won't be committed to repository

sensitive_data_exposed.impact: Session hijacking - attacker can impersonate authenticated users
Exposes authentication cookies, tracking cookies, and any other cookie data
OAuth code parameter can be used to obtain access tokens (lines 50-68 show OAuth flow with $_GET['code'])
Exposes oauth2state and any other session data

attack_scenario: 2. Log file is served by web server (no .htaccess or web.config rules block it)
3. Attacker reads session IDs from logged data
4. Attacker uses Cookie header with stolen PHPSESSID to hijack admin session
5. If OAuth codes are fresh, attacker can exchange them for access tokens (valid 90 seconds per OAuth spec)
6. Attacker gains full access to submission portal and Microsoft OAuth-protected resources

web_config_analysis.content_summary: Contains mime types, error handlers, URL rewrite rules, but NO rules to block .log file access
web_config_analysis.missing_protection: No <requestFiltering> or <hiddenSegments> rules to block .log files

php_code_analysis.logging_happens_at: Line 14 - BEFORE any authentication or authorization checks
php_code_analysis.oauth_flow: Lines 27-77 implement OAuth but don't protect the log file itself
php_code_analysis.no_access_control: No code checks user permissions before writing to or serving the log file

oauth_code_exposure_details.token_exposure: If OAuth code is logged before being exchanged, attacker can race to use it
oauth_code_exposure_details.timing_window: Authorization codes typically valid for 60-90 seconds

remediation: IMMEDIATE: Add .htaccess rule to block .log files: <FilesMatch "\.log$"> Deny from all </FilesMatch>
IMMEDIATE: Move log file outside web root (e.g., /var/log/ or ../logs/)
IMMEDIATE: Delete existing admin_debug.log file if it exists
URGENT: Remove debug logging entirely from production code (lines 14-21, 84-95)
URGENT: Implement proper error logging with rotation and restricted permissions
RECOMMENDED: Use PHP error_log() to system log instead of file_put_contents to web-accessible location
